supply_chain.dependencies: Development activities conducted through wholly-owned entities or joint ventures with pre-purchase transaction partners; fixed price construction contracts with unrelated parties to minimize construction cost risk
supply_chain.risks: Labor shortages, supply chain disruptions, escalating labor and material costs, shortages of skilled tradespeople and building components and materials, work stoppages, labor disputes

regulatory_risks: Compliance with federal, state and local laws and regulations including landlord-tenant laws, employment laws, antitrust and competition laws, privacy laws, tax laws, environmental laws, zoning laws, building codes

New or changed legal requirements including tenant screening limitations, affordable housing mandates, increased eviction notice periods, mandatory alternative dispute resolution, access to legal counsel for unrepresented tenants
Rent control or rent stabilization laws and regulations and other tenants' rights laws and regulations
Changes in tax laws and housing laws
Green building codes and laws addressing climate change effects requiring increased energy efficiency and greenhouse gas emissions performance
New data privacy laws such as the Texas Data Privacy and Security Act
Changes in REIT qualification requirements under the Internal Revenue Code
State and local income, excise and franchise taxes
Environmental regulations including removal or remediation of hazardous or toxic substances, underground storage tank regulations, asbestos regulations
Potential changes in federal income tax laws applicable to REITs
